doc_button_a11y_keyboard: Executed using the Space or Enter keys.
doc_button_a11y_role: Uses the standard <button> element with role="button" automatically assigned.
doc_button_anatomy_container: Container: Manages the button's boundary, padding, and rounded corners.
doc_button_anatomy_icon: Icon: Supplements the label. Can be placed before or after.
doc_button_anatomy_label: Label: Text indicating the action. Centered alignment is standard.
doc_button_best_practice_1: In principle, limit to one Primary button per visual hierarchy or modal.
doc_button_best_practice_2: Use specific verbs for labels to help users predict the outcome of their action.
doc_button_check1: Does it convey the result (benefit) after clicking?
doc_button_check2: Are you avoiding generic words like "OK" or "Yes"?
doc_button_check3: Does the button make sense by itself without context?
doc_button_choice_matrix_desc: Select the most appropriate button type based on the context.
doc_button_choice_primary_when: The main action on the screen, or the single completion button.
doc_button_choice_secondary_when: Auxiliary actions, secondary options paired with Primary (Cancel/Back).
doc_button_choice_tertiary_when: Subtle or supplementary actions in dense UIs.
doc_button_click_area_desc:
Ensure the click area is large enough to prevent misclicks
Especially consider finger operations on mobile devices
doc_button_considerations_visibility_des:
Make it stand out so users can find it quickly
Increase contrast between background and text
doc_button_consistency_desc:
Keep a consistent design for the same types of buttons to avoid user confusion
Recommended to use the same styles across the site
doc_button_content_concise: Keep labels concise and ideally displayed on a single line.
doc_button_design_intent: Buttons are the primary interface for users to explicitly perform actions (submit, save, delete, navigate) on a screen. Through visual weighting (Priority), they aim to smoothly guide the user to the next step.
doc_button_ex3_eg: When shortening "Save it" to "Save" to fit mobile footers
doc_button_feedback_desc:
Provide feedback upon clicking so users can confirm their action
Use color changes or animations
doc_button_guide_link: Guide: Refer to Guides / Link vs Button for when to use <button> vs <a>.
doc_button_icon_after_desc: Used for actions with directionality like "Next" or "Submit" to express progression.
doc_button_icon_before_desc: The most common placement. Enhances visibility and visually supplements the action's meaning.
doc_button_icon_only_case_desc: Used for widely recognized features (Search, Settings, etc.) to achieve a clean look. Alternative text (aria-label) is required for accessibility.
doc_button_icons_only_desc:
Indicates a specific action
Use only if the icon is self-explanatory
doc_button_icons_with_text_desc: A button combining text and an icon
doc_button_ja_notes:
Notes on Languages:
Too polite expressions might drop visibility. Keep it concise.
doc_button_label_desc:
Use concise and clear words to convey what happens next
Example: Submit, Confirm, Cancel
doc_button_label_guidelines_desc: Use "Verbs" for button labels. This clarifies "what will happen" after clicking.
doc_button_meaning_desc:
Limit to around 3 colors to avoid confusion
Provide icons too, since color-blind users may not distinguish color alone
doc_button_motion_active: Slight scaling (scale(0.98)) on click reproduces a physical push sensation.
doc_button_motion_hover: Subtle changes in background brightness indicate interactive feedback.
doc_button_noun_impact: May delay judgment of whether it is an action or a state
doc_button_primary_desc:
Indicates the main action, designed to stand out more than other buttons
Indicates the action you want users to take next
The most desired action on the screen
doc_button_scenario_form: Form Submission: Primary button placed at the bottom right of a modal.
doc_button_scenario_toolbar: Toolbar: Compact layout with icon-only buttons to save space.
doc_button_secondary_desc: Indicates an auxiliary action, designed to be less prominent than the primary button
doc_button_secondary_eg: "Cancel" (paired with primary, dialogue, major changes) or "Back", etc.
doc_button_size_desc_l:
Thumb-friendly size for mobile
Important buttons like CTA (landing pages) or Login
doc_button_size_desc_s:
Suitable size for mobile lists
In tables, sidebars, dense UIs, etc.
doc_button_state_active_desc:
Behavior when clicked
Conveys the pressing action
doc_button_state_disabled_desc:
Makes it inoperable
Conveys that it cannot be clicked
doc_button_state_focus_desc:
Behavior during keyboard operation
Conveys that it is the active target
doc_button_state_hover_desc:
Behavior when hovered
Conveys that it can be clicked
doc_button_state_loading_desc:
Makes it inoperable until processing finishes
Conveys it cannot be clicked until back to normal
Examples: "Loading...", "Processing...", "Saving..."
doc_button_states_desc: Unless intentionally animated, keep the size constant so layout shifts do not occur
doc_button_tertiary_desc:
Appears broken when placed next to primary/secondary without a background
Provides a clear click area when the background is shown
Like a "Text Link" or a "Ghost Button" where the background appears only on hover
doc_button_tertiary_eg: "Edit", "Cancel" (minor changes), "Link to submenu", "Help", "Share to SNS", "Add new", "Upload photo", "See details", etc.
doc_button_var_pri_dest_act: Particularly dangerous operations (Account deletion, clear all data)
doc_button_var_pri_dest_desc: Stands out the most, strongly warning the user "Are you sure?".
doc_button_var_sec_dest_act: General deletions (Remove one from list, delete a message, etc.)
doc_button_var_sec_dest_desc:
Not too prominent but still indicates "dangerous action"
Adjust size to fit properly with border thickness
doc_button_var_ter_dest_act: Subtle deletions (Delete at the corner of settings, minor clear)
doc_button_var_ter_dest_desc: When you don't want it to interfere with other options, but want to alert the user when noticed.
doc_button_variations_desc: Ensure button height remains the same across variations
doc_button_verb_impact: Purpose is clear at a glance
doc_buttongroup_a11y_spec: Assign role="group" to the container and set an aria-label to explain the purpose. Each button must be keyboard accessible.
doc_buttongroup_anatomy_buttons: Individual Buttons: Each element in the group. In joined mode, they share boundaries.
doc_buttongroup_anatomy_container: Container: Holds the buttons and defines the outer frame and background.
doc_buttongroup_basic_desc: Places buttons horizontally. You can adjust the spacing using the gap property.
doc_buttongroup_best_practice_1: Group only related actions together; unrelated actions should be separate buttons.
doc_buttongroup_best_practice_2: Avoid using primary variant for all buttons in a group — use secondary or tertiary to reduce cognitive load.
doc_buttongroup_choice_group_when: When multiple actions are closely related (e.g., Undo/Redo, Align Left/Center/Right) and you want to visually unify them.
doc_buttongroup_choice_matrix_desc: Choose based on the relevance of actions and the visual weight on the UI.
doc_buttongroup_choice_segmented_when: When presenting mutually exclusive options (tabs-like behavior) and wanting to maintain the current selection state.
doc_buttongroup_choice_single_when: When each action is independent and has a different context.
doc_buttongroup_content_guidelines: Use a consistent labeling style for buttons in the group. Actively use icons to save space.
doc_buttongroup_design_intent: Grouped buttons present a set of parallel actions or related options. By visually joining them, you communicate to the user that they are a single logical group.
doc_buttongroup_joined_desc:
Setting the joined property to true joins the buttons without gaps.
In this mode, the rounded corners of intermediate buttons are removed for a unified design.
doc_buttongroup_long_desc: The ButtonGroup component is used to group and display multiple buttons.
doc_buttongroup_motion_spec: Visual effects for hover and active states are applied to individual buttons but designed not to spoil the group's unity.
doc_buttongroup_override_desc:
By specifying the priority property, you can unify the priority of all buttons in the group.
Especially when using joined mode, mixed priorities can break the design.
doc_buttongroup_scenario_pagination: Pagination: Unifying Prev, Next, and page numbers into one set.
doc_buttongroup_scenario_toggle: View Toggle: Instantly switching between display formats like List and Grid.
doc_buttongroup_scenario_toolbar: Editor Toolbar: Grouping decoration buttons like Bold, Italic, Underline.
doc_buttongroup_sizes_desc: Examples of placing Large, Medium, and Small buttons.
doc_buttongroup_ux_note_desc:
As a UI design principle, it is recommended to have only one primary button per screen (or context).
If you use priority="primary" for multiple buttons, it increases cognitive load.

When unifying a group, choose secondary or tertiary. primary is possible but should be done with care.
doc_copybutton_a11y_desc: Follows WAI-ARIA guidelines with proper roles, labels, and keyboard navigation support.
doc_copybutton_anatomy_content: Content: The primary area where main content or children are rendered.
doc_copybutton_anatomy_root: Root: The outermost container managing state and layout.
doc_copybutton_best_practice_1: Use CopyButton consistently across your application to maintain a predictable user experience.
doc_copybutton_best_practice_2: Test CopyButton behavior across different screen sizes and input methods to ensure accessibility.
doc_copybutton_choice_alt_when: When a simpler alternative like Button better suits the use case.
doc_copybutton_choice_matrix_desc: Select the most appropriate component based on your requirements.
doc_copybutton_choice_self_when: When CopyButton functionality with standard options is required.
doc_copybutton_comparison_2: Ideal for copying code snippets or unique IDs.
doc_copybutton_comparison_desc: Unlike regular buttons, CopyButton automatically gives visual feedback (icons/messages) after copying.
doc_copybutton_content_guideline: Keep content concise and relevant to the component's purpose. Follow consistent labeling conventions across your application.
doc_copybutton_design_intent: CopyButton provides a consistent, reusable pattern for one-click clipboard copying with visual feedback, reducing implementation effort and ensuring design system compliance.
doc_copybutton_long_desc: Details and usage for the Copy Button component.
doc_copybutton_motion_desc: Transitions are minimal and purposeful, respecting the user's reduced-motion preferences.
doc_copybutton_scenario_1: Integrating CopyButton into workflows for data collection or display.
doc_copybutton_scenario_2: Using CopyButton in management views for information organization.
doc_floatbutton_a11y_desc: Follows WAI-ARIA guidelines with proper roles, labels, and keyboard navigation support.
doc_floatbutton_anatomy_content: Content: The primary area where main content or children are rendered.
doc_floatbutton_anatomy_root: Root: The outermost container managing state and layout.
doc_floatbutton_auto_shrink_desc: Float buttons auto-shrink based on scroll.
doc_floatbutton_backtop_desc: Quickly return to the top of the page when scrolled down.
doc_floatbutton_basic_usage: Standard implementation of FloatButton, typically positioned in a fixed corner of the screen.
doc_floatbutton_best_practice_1: Use FloatButton consistently across your application to maintain a predictable user experience.
doc_floatbutton_best_practice_2: Test FloatButton behavior across different screen sizes and input methods to ensure accessibility.
doc_floatbutton_choice_alt_when: When a simpler alternative like Button better suits the use case.
doc_floatbutton_choice_matrix_desc: Select the most appropriate component based on your requirements.
doc_floatbutton_choice_self_when: When FloatButton functionality with standard options is required.
doc_floatbutton_content_guideline: Keep content concise and relevant to the component's purpose. Follow consistent labeling conventions across your application.
doc_floatbutton_design_intent: FloatButton provides a consistent, reusable pattern for fixed-position floating action buttons for primary actions, reducing implementation effort and ensuring design system compliance.
doc_floatbutton_extended_desc: Float buttons can have extended text.
doc_floatbutton_extended_sizes_desc: Extended Float buttons are also available in different sizes.
doc_floatbutton_fixed_pos_desc: Define precise fixed coordinates for the floating button.
doc_floatbutton_group_desc: Organize multiple related actions into a single floating container.
doc_floatbutton_long_desc: Details and usage for the Float Button component.
doc_floatbutton_menu_desc: Expands child buttons when clicked or hovered, saving screen space while offering multiple choices.
doc_floatbutton_motion_desc: Transitions are minimal and purposeful, respecting the user's reduced-motion preferences.
doc_floatbutton_scenario_1: Integrating FloatButton into workflows for data collection or display.
doc_floatbutton_scenario_2: Using FloatButton in management views for information organization.
doc_floatbutton_sizes_desc: Float buttons are available in different sizes.
doc_floatbutton_tooltip_desc: Display a helpful description when hovering over the floating button.
doc_hamburgermenu_a11y_desc: Follows WAI-ARIA guidelines with proper roles, labels, and keyboard navigation support.
doc_hamburgermenu_anatomy_content: Content: The primary area where main content or children are rendered.
doc_hamburgermenu_anatomy_root: Root: The outermost container managing state and layout.
doc_hamburgermenu_best_practice_1: Use HamburgerMenu consistently across your application to maintain a predictable user experience.
doc_hamburgermenu_best_practice_2: Test HamburgerMenu behavior across different screen sizes and input methods to ensure accessibility.
doc_hamburgermenu_choice_alt_when: When a simpler alternative like Sidebar better suits the use case.
doc_hamburgermenu_choice_matrix_desc: Select the most appropriate component based on your requirements.
doc_hamburgermenu_choice_self_when: When HamburgerMenu functionality with standard options is required.
doc_hamburgermenu_content_guideline: Keep content concise and relevant to the component's purpose. Follow consistent labeling conventions across your application.
doc_hamburgermenu_design_intent: HamburgerMenu provides a consistent, reusable pattern for collapsible mobile navigation toggle menu, reducing implementation effort and ensuring design system compliance.
doc_hamburgermenu_motion_desc: Transitions are minimal and purposeful, respecting the user's reduced-motion preferences.
doc_hamburgermenu_scenario_1: Integrating HamburgerMenu into workflows for data collection or display.
doc_hamburgermenu_scenario_2: Using HamburgerMenu in management views for information organization.
doc_iconbutton_a11y_desc: Follows WAI-ARIA guidelines with proper roles, labels, and keyboard navigation support.
doc_iconbutton_anatomy_content: Content: The primary area where main content or children are rendered.
doc_iconbutton_anatomy_root: Root: The outermost container managing state and layout.
doc_iconbutton_best_practice_1: Use IconButton consistently across your application to maintain a predictable user experience.
doc_iconbutton_best_practice_2: Test IconButton behavior across different screen sizes and input methods to ensure accessibility.
doc_iconbutton_choice_alt_when: When a simpler alternative like Button better suits the use case.
doc_iconbutton_choice_matrix_desc: Select the most appropriate component based on your requirements.
doc_iconbutton_choice_self_when: When IconButton functionality with standard options is required.
doc_iconbutton_content_guideline: Keep content concise and relevant to the component's purpose. Follow consistent labeling conventions across your application.
doc_iconbutton_design_intent: IconButton provides a consistent, reusable pattern for icon-only button for compact, recognizable actions, reducing implementation effort and ensuring design system compliance.
doc_iconbutton_long_desc: A button component that displays only an icon.
doc_iconbutton_motion_desc: Transitions are minimal and purposeful, respecting the user's reduced-motion preferences.
doc_iconbutton_scenario_1: Integrating IconButton into workflows for data collection or display.
doc_iconbutton_scenario_2: Using IconButton in management views for information organization.
doc_iconbutton_title: A button component that displays only an icon instead of text.
doc_link_a11y_note_desc: Avoid ambiguous text like "Click here" or "Learn more". Instead, use specific descriptive phrases that clarify the destination (e.g., "View Pricing Plans"). This helps screen reader users understand where the link leads and strongly improves SEO.
doc_link_icons_desc: Icons can be placed on the left or right of the label.
doc_link_long_desc: The Link component provides text-based navigation or links to external resources. It supports internationalization, resizing, icon additions, and three levels of priority.
doc_link_priority_desc: Select from primary, secondary, or tertiary based on the link importance.
doc_link_priority_primary_desc: The most visible style. Used for primary actions like "View Pricing Plans".
doc_link_priority_secondary_desc: The standard text link style. Used for general navigation.
doc_link_priority_tertiary_desc: A subtle link style used for supplementary or lower-priority navigation items.
doc_link_priority_tertiary_style_desc: A subtle style with minimal decoration. Suitable for supplementary links or crowded areas.
doc_link_sizes_desc: Supports three sizes: small, medium, and large.
doc_link_vs_button_guide_desc: See Guides / Link vs Button for when to use `<a>` vs `<button>`.
doc_linkbutton_a11y_desc: Follows WAI-ARIA guidelines with proper roles, labels, and keyboard navigation support.
doc_linkbutton_anatomy_content: Content: The primary area where main content or children are rendered.
doc_linkbutton_anatomy_root: Root: The outermost container managing state and layout.
doc_linkbutton_best_practice_1: Use LinkButton consistently across your application to maintain a predictable user experience.
doc_linkbutton_best_practice_2: Test LinkButton behavior across different screen sizes and input methods to ensure accessibility.
doc_linkbutton_choice_alt_when: When a simpler alternative like Button better suits the use case.
doc_linkbutton_choice_matrix_desc: Select the most appropriate component based on your requirements.
doc_linkbutton_choice_self_when: When LinkButton functionality with standard options is required.
doc_linkbutton_content_guideline: Keep content concise and relevant to the component's purpose. Follow consistent labeling conventions across your application.
doc_linkbutton_design_intent: LinkButton provides a consistent, reusable pattern for button-styled element that navigates like a link, reducing implementation effort and ensuring design system compliance.
doc_linkbutton_long_desc: A button component that functions as a link.
doc_linkbutton_motion_desc: Transitions are minimal and purposeful, respecting the user's reduced-motion preferences.
doc_linkbutton_scenario_1: Integrating LinkButton into workflows for data collection or display.
doc_linkbutton_scenario_2: Using LinkButton in management views for information organization.
doc_linkbutton_title: A button that functions like a link, used for navigation or actions that look like links.
